exercises.title: Trace bounded type — which calls compile?
exercises.scenario: Bounds restrict which types can substitute for T.
exercises.instructions:
A method is declared: `static <T extends Number> double doubled(T n) { return n.doubleValue() * 2; }`

Which of these COMPILE? Write C or E for each:
```
A: doubled(42)
B: doubled(3.14)
C: doubled("hello")
D: doubled(100L)
E: doubled(true)
```
exercises.expected_solution:
A: C
B: C
C: E
D: C
E: E

test_cases.expected:
A: C
B: C
C: E
D: C
E: E

hints: Integer, Double, Long all extend Number → C
String does not extend Number → E
Boolean does not extend Number → E

exercises.title: Add Comparable bound
exercises.scenario: Bound must be added to use compareTo.
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank so `max()` can call `compareTo()` on T.

blanks.hint: In generics, always use extends — even for interfaces

hints: Generics use 'extends' for both class and interface bounds
Never use 'implements' in a type bound

exercises.title: Generic min method
exercises.instructions:
Write `min(List<T>)` returning the minimum element using compareTo. Test with integers and strings.

Expected output:
1
Aisha
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Generic min — T extends Comparable<T>
hints: static <T extends Comparable<T>> T min(List<T> list)
compareTo returns negative if item < result
Aisha is lexicographically smallest

exercises.title: Generic sum with Number bound
exercises.scenario: Sum any list of numbers using Number.doubleValue().
exercises.instructions:
Write `sum(List<T extends Number>)` returning double. Test with List<Integer>, List<Double>, List<Long>.

Expected output:
60.0
7.5
600.0
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Generic sum with Number bound
hints: static <T extends Number> double sum(List<T> numbers)
n.doubleValue() — Number method available on any T
Works for Integer, Double, Long — all extend Number

exercises.title: Generic clamp method
exercises.scenario: Keep a value within a range using Comparable.
exercises.instructions:
Write `clamp(T value, T min, T max)` — returns value clamped within [min, max].

Expected output:
10
5
0
Priya
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Generic clamp with Comparable bound
hints: static <T extends Comparable<T>> T clamp(T value, T min, T max)
compareTo < 0 means value < min; > 0 means value > max
Priya is alphabetically between Aisha and Raj

exercises.title: SortedBox<T extends Comparable<T>>
exercises.scenario: Generic class with Comparable bound keeps items sorted.
exercises.instructions:
Create `SortedBox<T extends Comparable<T>>` that maintains a sorted list. add(T), min(), max(), toList().

Expected output:
[Aisha, Priya, Raj]
Min: Aisha | Max: Raj
[1, 2, 3, 5, 8]

test_cases.expected_stdout:
[Aisha, Priya, Raj]
Min: Aisha | Max: Raj
[1, 2, 3, 5, 8]

hints: class SortedBox<T extends Comparable<T>>
Collections.sort(items) works because T is Comparable
After sort, first element is min, last is max

exercises.title: Generic isBetween method
exercises.scenario: Check if a value is within a range.
exercises.instructions:
Write `isBetween(T value, T low, T high)` returning boolean. Test with numbers and strings.

Expected output:
true
false
true
false
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Generic isBetween with Comparable bound
hints: value >= low AND value <= high using compareTo
compareTo >= 0 means value >= low

exercises.title: Generic average with Number bound
exercises.instructions:
Write `average(List<T extends Number>)` returning OptionalDouble (empty if list is empty). Test with three numeric types.

Expected output:
84.33333333333333
2.5
OptionalDouble.empty
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Generic average with Number bound
hints: Return OptionalDouble — handles empty case
sum / nums.size() for the average

exercises.title: Multiple bounds — Number AND Comparable
exercises.scenario: Dual bound grants both numeric operations and comparison.
exercises.instructions:
Write `median(List<T>)` where `T extends Number & Comparable<T>`. Sort the list (Comparable), then return middle element.

Expected output:
3
3.5
300
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// median with T extends Number & Comparable<T>
hints: T extends Number & Comparable<T> — both required
Collections.sort() needs Comparable (second bound)
Index size/2 gives middle element (for odd-length, exact middle)

exercises.title: Bound prevents wrong type at compile time
exercises.scenario: Compile error for types that don't satisfy the bound.
exercises.instructions:
Given: `static <T extends Comparable<T>> T max(List<T> list)`

Which calls COMPILE (C) and which give ERRORS (E)?

```
A: max(List.of(3, 1, 4))               // Integer
B: max(List.of("Raj", "Aisha"))        // String
C: max(List.of(new Object()))           // Object
D: max(List.of(3.14, 2.71))            // Double
E: max(List.of(new ArrayList<>()))     // ArrayList
```
exercises.expected_solution:
A: C
B: C
C: E
D: C
E: E

test_cases.expected:
A: C
B: C
C: E
D: C
E: E

hints: Integer, String, Double all implement Comparable → C
Object implements Comparable? No → E
ArrayList implements Comparable? No → E

exercises.title: Generic NumberBox with arithmetic
exercises.scenario: Bounded generic class with numeric operations.
exercises.instructions:
Create `NumberBox<T extends Number>` that stores a value and supports: doubleValue(), longValue(), isPositive(), isZero(), percentage(double total) = (value / total) * 100.

Test with Integer and Double.

Expected output:
IntBox: 42 as double: 42.0 | positive: true
DblBox: 7.5 as long: 7 | positive: true
42 is 21.0% of 200
7.5 is 25.0% of 30

test_cases.expected_stdout:
IntBox: 42 as double: 42.0 | positive: true
DblBox: 7.5 as long: 7 | positive: true
42 is 21.0% of 200
7.5 is 25.0% of 30

hints: class NumberBox<T extends Number>
All methods use value.doubleValue() or value.longValue()

exercises.title: Generic range check and sort
exercises.scenario: Methods using Comparable bound for range and sorting.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `RangeUtils` class with:
- `<T extends Comparable<T>> boolean overlaps(T aMin, T aMax, T bMin, T bMax)` — do ranges [a,a] and [b,b] overlap?
- `<T extends Comparable<T>> List<T> sorted(List<T>)` — return sorted copy

Expected output:
Ranges [1,5] and [3,7] overlap: true
Ranges [1,3] and [5,7] overlap: false
Sorted: [Aisha, Priya, Raj, Vikram]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// RangeUtils: overlaps and sorted

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Ranges [1,5] and [3,7] overlap: true
Ranges [1,3] and [5,7] overlap: false
Sorted: [Aisha, Priya, Raj, Vikram]

hints: Overlap condition: aMin <= bMax AND bMin <= aMax

exercises.title: Statistics<T extends Number & Comparable<T>>
exercises.scenario: The worked example — dual bound for full statistics.
exercises.instructions:
Implement the full `Statistics<T extends Number & Comparable<T>>` class: add(), count(), sum(), average(), min(), max(), range(), isBetween(T).

Test with exam scores and stock prices.

Expected output:
Count: 6, Sum: 506.0, Avg: 84.33
Min: 68, Max: 95, Range: 27.0
90 in range: true
Avg price: ₹2078.0
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Statistics<T extends Number & Comparable<T>> — full implementation

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Count: 6, Sum: 506.0, Avg: 84.33
Min: 68, Max: 95, Range: 27.0
90 in range: true
Avg price: ₹2078.0

hints: Dual bound: both Number (for doubleValue) and Comparable (for compareTo) methods available
range() uses both: max().doubleValue() - min().doubleValue()
sum of scores = 506, average = 84.333..., formatted to 2 decimals

exercises.title: Generic binary search
exercises.scenario: Binary search needs Comparable to compare elements.
exercises.instructions:
Implement `binarySearch(List<T>, T target)` where T extends Comparable<T>. Return the index (or -1 if not found). List must be sorted.

Expected output:
Index of 42: 4
Index of 100: -1
Index of Priya: 2
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// Generic binary search with Comparable bound

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Index of 42: 4
Index of 100: -1
Index of Priya: 2

hints: Binary search: compare mid element to target using compareTo
cmp < 0: target is in right half; cmp > 0: target is in left half
Sorted names: [Aisha, Priya, Raj, Vikram] — Priya at index 2

exercises.title: Complete bounded generics utility — Comparisons
exercises.scenario: Six methods using Comparable and Number bounds.
exercises.instructions:
Build `ComparableUtils` with six bounded generic methods:
1. `<T extends Comparable<T>> T max(List<T>)` — max of list
2. `<T extends Comparable<T>> T min(List<T>)` — min of list
3. `<T extends Comparable<T>> T clamp(T v, T lo, T hi)` — keep in range
4. `<T extends Comparable<T>> List<T> sorted(List<T>)` — sorted copy
5. `<T extends Number> double sum(List<T>)` — sum
6. `<T extends Number & Comparable<T>> T medianOf(T a, T b, T c)` — middle of 3 values

Test all 6 with appropriate data.

Expected output:
Max: 95
Min: 68
Clamp 150 in [0,100]: 100
Sorted: [Aisha, Priya, Raj]
Sum: 506.0
Median of 8,3,5: 5
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;
// ComparableUtils — 6 bounded generic methods

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Max: 95
Min: 68
Clamp 150 in [0,100]: 100
Sorted: [Aisha, Priya, Raj]
Sum: 506.0
Median of 8,3,5: 5

hints: medianOf: sort [8, 3, 5] → [3, 5, 8] → middle = 5
Each method has its own appropriate bound
